reasoning_content:
We need to determine if 263 is a prime number. A prime number is greater than 1 and has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. We can check divisibility by small primes.

First, note that 263 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 2+6+3=11, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Ends with 3, not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*37=259, 7*38=266, so 263 not divisible by 7. Check 11: 11*23=253, 11*24=264, so no. Check 13: 13*20=260, 13*21=273, so no. Check 17: 17*15=255, 17*16=272, so no. Check 19: 19*13=247, 19*14=266, so no. Check 23: 23*11=253, 23*12=276, so no. The square root of 263 is about 16.2, so we only need to check primes up to 13 (since 17 > sqrt(263)). Actually sqrt(263) ~ 16.2, so primes up to 13 are sufficient. We checked 2,3,5,7,11,13 all not dividing. So 263 is prime.

Answer: yes.
